Hi there, thanks for joining our team! By now, you already know that Bridie Farrell — former U.S. National Team speed skater and advocate for crime victims — is taking on far-right Rep. Elise Stefanik for Upstate New York’s 21st District in the U.S. House of Representatives. And she’s ready to get to work. **[Check out Bridie’s official launch video here, then pitch in ANY amount to fuel our campaign!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38526447/315846819/-1129275370?refcode=fl_email_fr_welcome1_tb_new_xx_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTU5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMmM1N2M0Zi0wMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=omypCiS7k9JAr7eEI3XZbmyg0IVQNi1Qa1qQ_trDBmc=)** | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38526449/315846821/-1129275370?refcode=fl_email_fr_welcome1_tb_new_xx_img&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTU5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMmM1N2M0Zi0wMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=omypCiS7k9JAr7eEI3XZbmyg0IVQNi1Qa1qQ_trDBmc=)

Bridie knows what it means to work hard, fight for what’s right, and make your voice heard.
Bridie is a national voice for victims of crime. She founded a nonprofit organization, America Loves Kids, that supports survivors of abuse and works with policymakers to enact programs that benefit crime victims. She was the leading advocate for passage of the Child Victims Act (CVA) in New York.
Now law on the books, CVA allows abuse survivors like Bridie to hold abusers and institutions accountable. Since passing CVA in New York, Bridie has worked with Republicans and Democrats across the country to pass similar legislation in other states. That’s who Bridie is — a person who gets things done right.
Not only does Bridie have the dedication and relentless work ethic to be a powerful voice for the residents of Northern NY’s 21st District, but she also brings the right ideas and values to this race. She was born and raised in the North Country. And she has made clear to all of us that this race isn’t about her. It is about you.
In the U.S. House of Representatives, Bridie will stand up for better jobs, a cleaner environment, broadband access for the North Country, public safety, and improved educational opportunities for everyone. She will bring the values she learned growing up in the Adirondack Mountains to Washington, and she won’t forget them once she gets there.
**Going up against the 3rd ranking member in the House Minority leadership is no small challenge, but with enough grassroots support from folks like you, Bridie can overcome the odds and become the Congresswoman that the North Country and all Americans need and deserve.**
**[Will you show Bridie your support by chipping in $10 or more right now? We know she’ll appreciate it!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38526451/315846823/-1129275370?refcode=fl_email_fr_welcome1_tb_new_xx_l2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTU5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMmM1N2M0Zi0wMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=omypCiS7k9JAr7eEI3XZbmyg0IVQNi1Qa1qQ_trDBmc=)**

Peter,

Today marks Veterans Day 2021. It’s a day to commemorate and show appreciation for our veterans and recognize their contributions to the defense and protection of the foundations of our democracy.

Today is also a day to recognize that time and again, we have failed our veterans. We’ve failed to provide them with the resources they need to acclimate themselves into our everyday society.

From health care to job opportunities, from housing to mental health services -- our veterans deserve more.

**Today and every day, we honor our veterans and fight for them in every way we can. Let us take today to recommit ourselves to fighting for the things our veterans have long deserved.**

**[In honor of Veteran’s Day, we’re sending a card to veterans in Florida. Sign the card now -- you can even add a personalized message or shout out a veteran in your own life.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38526633/315847590/1499584895?ms=em21_211111_vetscard&refcode=em21_211111_vetscard&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MjI4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNjk1ZjY2Zi0wMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hY43dwxI_fIjQepFs_kBlpwDlBu0m8GDvTeOqAregMI=&emci=723edc5b-6e41-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=d695f66f-0143-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=34271788)**

clean_body: | = Peter, Today marks Veterans Day 2021. It’s a day to commemorate and show appreciation for our veterans and recognize their contributions to the defense and protection of the foundations of our democracy. Today is also a day to recognize that time and again, we have failed our veterans. We’ve failed to provide them with the resources they need to acclimate themselves into our everyday society. From health care to job opportunities, from housing to mental health services -- our veterans deserve more. Today and every day, we honor our veterans and fight for them in every way we can. Let us take today to recommit ourselves to fighting for the things our veterans have long deserved. | \-- Team Rayner = *

name: Eric Lynn
subject: Happy Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Lynn for Congress
body:
I’m proud to support our veterans https://www.ericlynnforcongress.com [https://www.ericlynnforcongress.com]Dear friends,
Today is Veterans Day, an important time to commemorate the men and women who have served our country.
Our service members have made untold sacrifices to keep our country safe and ensure we can maintain the American way of life.
My time in President Obama’s Department of Defense offered me the opportunity to work closely with many members of the military and see firsthand the issues they and their families face while in the service and after departure or retirement.
That’s why I worked to set up the opportunity to help active service members with a little known challenge: buying a home for their families. Laws and regulations require that if a married couple is purchasing a home, both spouses needed to sign the paperwork in person, which proved very difficult for those serving overseas. Our program allows service members, veterans and their spouses to each sign securely online, previously not allowed here in Florida. This has helped Soldiers, Sailors, Airmen, and Marines so that their spouses can move into their new homes without further delay.
I’m also proud to serve as a Veteran Advocate in Pinellas County, helping veterans on a pro-bono basis with advocacy for benefits, healthcare, and other needs.
https://ericlynnforcongress.com [https://ericlynnforcongress.com]I work every day to ensure our service members and veterans have the resources and services they need, and I look forward to continuing that work.
Today, let’s honor our veterans and take a moment to appreciate their contributions to the nation we love.
Happy Veterans Day!
Eric Lynn

name: Josh Harder
subject: It's our duty to stand up for our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Harder for Congress
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/zkCx6e6A9dpst3iE5sw327O0jihA0VQdESeGrwsbnO4=/MKF-SQV78--KvAw7dl1QacueRDl2CHtQF4lOc3ae1Xo=?refcode=E12372-20211111-VeteransDay-header-Holiday-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=20) | Peter - Not just today on Veterans Day, but every day, we are duty-bound to honor and thank our friends, family and community members who have so bravely served in our armed forces. For me, like so many, today is a personal one. My grandfather served two tours in Vietnam and told me stories growing up of his tours abroad. But his life was cut short due to his exposure to Agent Orange when he was overseas. It's because of him that I proudly led the charge in Congress to get our veterans exposed to Agent Orange access to their full benefits. And we still have a long way to go. Housing, healthcare, education - all these are areas where our country is falling short in providing our veterans with the benefits they've earned. We can never fully repay the men and women who put their lives on the line for our country, but we sure as heck can keep working to get closer. To our veterans and their families -- thank you for your sacrifices. In gratitude, Josh
---

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/zkCx6e6A9dpst3iE5sw327O0jihA0VQdESeGrwsbnO4=/UhTb_qtk68dq-1C7eyRUyaaF7jIDUCtQDQdymDFc7-0=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/zkCx6e6A9dpst3iE5sw327O0jihA0VQdESeGrwsbnO4=/VGZZrG2fV-QIvTFIhJYAsaqClUp5GtoI5u8YBorUzqU=)

name: Barrier to Entry PAC
subject: NEW ENDORSEMENT ALERT: Meet Jessica Mason
body:
Veteran’s Day seemed like a fitting day to introduce TX-30’s New Deal

| | | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/38527198/315850098/214728439?refcode=211111_FR_jessicamasonspotlight_img1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Mzc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImRiMmFhNTlmLTAyNDMtZWMxMS05ODIwLWM4OTY2NTNiMjZjOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=IDsgQ2IGYtYRWYaSowo9ARIhyD_86bnmfv0K9Sq98yc=)
---
Peter,

Today is Veteran’s Day, which felt like the perfect day to officially announce our endorsement of **Jessica Mason for Congress in Texas’ 30th District!** [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/38527199/315850099/214728439?refcode=211111_FR_jessicamasonspotlight_img2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Mzc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImRiMmFhNTlmLTAyNDMtZWMxMS05ODIwLWM4OTY2NTNiMjZjOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=IDsgQ2IGYtYRWYaSowo9ARIhyD_86bnmfv0K9Sq98yc=) Jessica Mason is a veteran who served for 5 years in the Navy as a medic and mental health counselor, witnessing all the tolls of war. Her husband is also on active duty. Jessica was born and raised in Dallas and is the strong progressive that Texas 30th Congressional District needs.

From growing up in poverty to enlisting in the military so that she could complete her degree, Jessica knows first-hand what it means to be from a community that was set up to fail by design due to socioeconomic barriers.

Unlike many of the veterans in politics who come from affluent backgrounds, Jessica reflects the underrepresented majority of service members who are poor and working-class. She gives a critical voice to those of our veterans who enlisted in the military out of economic necessity and return home only to be neglected by our failing VA system.

Jessica has dedicated her life to her community and to public service. After her time in the military, Jessica obtained her Master’s of Public Administration while working as a legislative aide in the Virginia State Legislature. Since then, she has been working as an affordable housing administrator and anti-poverty activist, while volunteering year-round.

Now, Jessica Mason is running for Congress because, as a true Daughter of Dallas, this fight is deeply personal to her. She’s tired of seeing her friends, her family, and her neighbors hurting.

When she makes it to Congress, Jessica will fight for progressive causes like investments in our schools, universal healthcare, reproductive freedom, criminal justice reform, affordable housing solutions, labor union rights, climate justice, and economic equality.

Jessica is running a grassroots campaign that rejects corporate PAC money because she refuses to let corporate greed influence her like it has for far too many corrupt politicians.

We are so proud to endorse Jessica Mason as a Barrier To Entry candidate and to throw our full support behind her. With less than 100 days until her primary election, we need to make sure she has the resources and funds necessary to win. **[Will you rush a contribution to help us elect Jessica Mason as the bold progressive leader that TX-30 needs?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/38527200/315850100/214728439?refcode=211111_FR_jessicamasonspotlight_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Mzc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImRiMmFhNTlmLTAyNDMtZWMxMS05ODIwLWM4OTY2NTNiMjZjOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=IDsgQ2IGYtYRWYaSowo9ARIhyD_86bnmfv0K9Sq98yc=)** **______[DONATE TO JESSICA →](https://click.everyaction.com/k/38527201/315850101/214728439?refcode=211111_FR_jessicamasonspotlight_B1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Mzc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImRiMmFhNTlmLTAyNDMtZWMxMS05ODIwLWM4OTY2NTNiMjZjOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=IDsgQ2IGYtYRWYaSowo9ARIhyD_86bnmfv0K9Sq98yc=)** Thanks for helping us elect working-class veterans, **_Barrier To Entry PAC_**
**BARRIER TO ENTRY PAC supports candidates and campaign staff from working class backgrounds as they carve their paths in the Democratic political space. We provide direct cash assistance for essential items like phones, cars, laptops, and living stipends -- which often times, these candidates and their campaigns don't have access to. We also serve as a resource for individual mentorship and training to ensure candidates and their staff feel supported and equipped to enter the political space.** _****Pitch in today to help provide necessary support and resources for working class candidates today! >>****_ **______[DONATE TO BTE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/38527202/315850102/1300596418?refcode=211111_FR_jessicamasonspotlight_biobox&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Mzc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImRiMmFhNTlmLTAyNDMtZWMxMS05ODIwLWM4OTY2NTNiMjZjOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=IDsgQ2IGYtYRWYaSowo9ARIhyD_86bnmfv0K9Sq98yc=)**

name: Liz Mathis
subject: Join me today in honoring Iowa veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Liz Mathis for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38527409/315850909/-1029444312?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTUxNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNDQ5ZjQwYS0wMzQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=z_qHzoDucZaQ-bC0riWJqiCy2kj42D2D7swCTs5ZLQQ=&emci=0351074c-6e42-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=f449f40a-0343-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=6566505) Hi Peter,

Today, we honor veterans for their sacrifice and service to this country. By choosing to enlist, these brave men and women have put their lives on the line to protect the freedom and security that are at the heart of our democracy.

As the daughter of a World War II veteran, I know firsthand the sacrifices that veterans and their families have made in the name of our democracy. I saw how difficult it is to transition to a life back in the States, and how critical it is that we provide extra support to our veterans.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38527410/315850910/-1029444312?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTUxNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNDQ5ZjQwYS0wMzQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=z_qHzoDucZaQ-bC0riWJqiCy2kj42D2D7swCTs5ZLQQ=&emci=0351074c-6e42-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=f449f40a-0343-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=6566505)
_My father Jim in uniform._ From the expansion of healthcare to improved VA benefits, veterans deserve to come home and be treated with dignity and respect. As a member of the Veterans Affairs Committee in the Iowa State Senate, I have fought for Iowa veterans, and I want to bring that experience to Congress.

I hope you will join me in taking some time today to thank a veteran for their service, and pledging to support them and their families even after their service has ended. Thank you,

Liz Mathis
_Candidate for Congress, IA-02_
| | Liz Mathis is running to represent Iowa's 2nd Congressional District. clean_body: Hi Peter, Today, we honor veterans for their sacrifice and service to this country. By choosing to enlist, these brave men and women have put their lives on the line to protect the freedom and security that are at the heart of our democracy. As the daughter of a World War II veteran, I know firsthand the sacrifices that veterans and their families have made in the name of our democracy. I saw how difficult it is to transition to a life back in the States, and how critical it is that we provide extra support to our veterans. My father Jim in uniform. From the expansion of healthcare to improved VA benefits, veterans deserve to come home and be treated with dignity and respect. As a member of the Veterans Affairs Committee in the Iowa State Senate, I have fought for Iowa veterans, and I want to bring that experience to Congress. I hope you will join me in taking some time today to thank a veteran for their service, and pledging to support them and their families even after their service has ended. Thank you, Liz Mathis Candidate for Congress, IA-02 | | Liz Mathis is running to represent Iowa's 2nd Congressional District. name: John King
subject: My uncle → Lieutenant Colonel Haldane King
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John King for Governor, Eliza Leighton, Treasurer
body:
On this Veterans Day, I hope you’ll take a moment to reflect on the strength of those who have served.

| Peter,

Today is Veterans Day, a time to honor and reflect on the sacrifice our servicemen and women make serving our country every day.

**Today, I’m thinking about my uncle, Lieutenant Colonel Haldane King — a Tuskegee Airman. He overcame intense discrimination to become one of the first Black combat pilots in the Army and went on to be a firefighter and later a career Air Force officer.**

After my parents passed away and I got kicked out of high school, I went to live with my Uncle Hal. At a time in my life when I felt lost and confused, he not only gave me structure and stability, but he gave me the push I needed to take ownership of my life. I’ll never forget when he told me: **“Neither you nor I can control the things that happened to you as a child, but you are responsible for the shape of your life going forward.”**
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528067/315854761/-923212789?refcode=BLU-KING-20211111-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzM2IzNTY2Ni0wNjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I3bI-rp57Ib2Fdt6ubb8E1J8qCNB_x_6c0n5hCRXSzY=)
I’m channeling my uncle’s wisdom and spirit of service in my fight to become Maryland’s next governor. On this Veterans Day, I hope you’ll take a moment to reflect on the strength of those who have served.

Thank you,

John

name: Sara Jacobs

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
Thank you to everyone who served our country.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528278/315856236/-1384613475?refcode=BLU-SJC-20211111-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTMxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGUzNDMxMS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ae08tV0y1jRhrbsYOI4dyRsghcNopKxrF-A-3FqBxcM=)

| Today, I’m honoring our veterans and their families in San Diego and across the country.

Representing a proud military community — home to over 240,000 veterans — I am honored to fight for our veterans every day in Congress. That’s why I’ve introduced bipartisan legislation to end veterans' homelessness, which will have a huge impact on our community here in San Diego.

Thank you to everyone who served our country. Let’s honor them by building a country that is worthy of their sacrifice.

With gratitude,
Sara

P.S. **If you are a veteran struggling with mental health, please know that[ _resources are available._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528279/315856237/-273040098?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTMxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGUzNDMxMS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ae08tV0y1jRhrbsYOI4dyRsghcNopKxrF-A-3FqBxcM=&emci=fda83aaa-4f42-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=28e34311-0843-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=14239920)**

name: Ben Samuels
subject: Honor and service
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ben Samuels for Congress
body:
We owe our veterans so much more than annual gratitude.

_[Click here to unsubscribe from this email list](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/eb3e7e92-ff42-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8/28ba7438-0843-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTUyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGJhNzQzOC0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AdqWSLDlSfWd7QhH4zMtKgRDe63-qokyd4b2VAG6Oco=&id=116681400). Or to receive fewer emails, [click here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528362/315856617/-423620563?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTUyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGJhNzQzOC0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AdqWSLDlSfWd7QhH4zMtKgRDe63-qokyd4b2VAG6Oco=&emci=eb3e7e92-ff42-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=28ba7438-0843-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=14943358)._
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528371/315856631/-1223207246?refcode=EM_FR_211111VeteransDay_H_BS_90DO_3_G_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTUyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGJhNzQzOC0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AdqWSLDlSfWd7QhH4zMtKgRDe63-qokyd4b2VAG6Oco=)
Today, we honor the service members who have kept our country safe. Veterans, as well as their spouses, kids, and extended family, make physical and economic sacrifices for all of us that can truly never be repaid.
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528373/315856635/1413578467?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTUyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGJhNzQzOC0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AdqWSLDlSfWd7QhH4zMtKgRDe63-qokyd4b2VAG6Oco=&emci=eb3e7e92-ff42-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=28ba7438-0843-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=14943358)**
I’m proud of my family’s service and military history. My grandfather, Buddy, fought in World War II and earned a Purple Heart for his service during Iwo Jima. When he came back from the war, he gave back to our community by diving headfirst into my family’s business, the Samuels Shoe Company. I try to live by his example and I’m committed to doing whatever I can to give back to our community.
Today, as we show gratitude to the men and women who have protected our nation, I hope we all feel inspired to dive headfirst into community service to pay homage to their sacrifices.
Thanks for doing your part,
Ben Samuels
Ben Samuels is a Democrat running in Missouri’s Second Congressional District. As a sixth-generation Missourian, economic policy expert, and businessperson, Ben will work tirelessly to deliver results for the community his family has long called home. **This election will be tough, but with the help of grassroots donors like you -- anything is possible.** **______[DONATE MONTHLY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528375/315856637/-1223207246?refcode=EM_FR_211111VeteransDay_H_BS_90DO_3_G_N_BIO&recurring=auto&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTUyMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGJhNzQzOC0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AdqWSLDlSfWd7QhH4zMtKgRDe63-qokyd4b2VAG6Oco=)**

clean_body: We owe our veterans so much more than annual gratitude. . Or to receive fewer emails, . = Today, we honor the service members who have kept our country safe. Veterans, as well as their spouses, kids, and extended family, make physical and economic sacrifices for all of us that can truly never be repaid. I’m proud of my family’s service and military history. My grandfather, Buddy, fought in World War II and earned a Purple Heart for his service during Iwo Jima. When he came back from the war, he gave back to our community by diving headfirst into my family’s business, the Samuels Shoe Company. I try to live by his example and I’m committed to doing whatever I can to give back to our community. Today, as we show gratitude to the men and women who have protected our nation, I hope we all feel inspired to dive headfirst into community service to pay homage to their sacrifices. Thanks for doing your part, Ben Samuels Ben Samuels is a Democrat running in Missouri’s Second Congressional District. As a sixth-generation Missourian, economic policy expert, and businessperson, Ben will work tirelessly to deliver results for the community his family has long called home. This election will be tough, but with the help of grassroots donors like you -- anything is possible. __= || || Ben Samuels for Congress 9621 Olive Blvd. P.O. Box 8484 Saint Louis, MO 63132-9998 United States | Paid for by Ben Samuels for Congress Contributions or gifts to Ben Samuels for Congress are not tax deductible. name: Kara Hahn
subject: Why I'm running
body:
|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528720/315858724/922507959?refcode=em21_emailheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=)

Hey there --

I'm Kara Hahn, a proud Democrat born and raised right here in Suffolk County. After nearly a decade representing this community in the County Legislature, **I'm proud to be running for Congress in New York's 1st Congressional District.**

As the daughter of a teacher and volunteer firefighter, I learned early what it means to show up for your community. It's this example that led me to pursue a career in social work, and to then take on local issues as a Suffolk County Legislator. And now, it's what's driving me to step up in this moment and ensure we have strong representation in Congress.

For too long, Long Islanders have been held back without supportive leadership in Congress. Under the tenure of Lee Zeldin, our kids can't afford a home in the neighborhood where they grew up, we've struggled to afford the cost of health care and prescription medication, and we've been dragged into repeated partisan grudge matches that in no way reflect the values of our communities.

**That's why I'm running: it's time we have solutions-oriented leadership working to secure the support and relief we need to thrive.**

**[But we can't achieve progress without your help, friend -- will you become a supporter of my campaign and get us started off on the right foot?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528724/315858729/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=)** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [Express Donate $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528727/315858733/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&express_lane=true&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=) | [Express Donate $25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528728/315858739/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&express_lane=true&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=)
---|---
[Express Donate $50](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528731/315858740/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&express_lane=true&amount=50&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=) | [Express Donate $100](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528733/315858741/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&express_lane=true&amount=100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=)
[Express Donate $250](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528736/315858742/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&express_lane=true&amount=250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=) | [Donate Other Amount](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528737/315858745/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=)
The last thing Long Islanders need is another self-promoting, hot-head playing politics with our vote in Congress. I care about this community -- and I care deeply about its people. I want to roll up my sleeves and do the hard work to solve the problems we are facing.

We can have so much better than this tired old status quo that consistently leaves working families behind -- we are in this together and our first step forward is taking this district back for our communities.

**With Zeldin vacating this seat, we have an unparalleled opportunity to win progress for families and working people. To improve our schools and reduce the cost of our health care. To protect our environment and create good-paying jobs.**

Like many Long Islanders, I've never backed down from the challenges life throws at you, and this campaign will be no exception. I'm committed to doing everything I can to build a strong campaign, but I'm going to need your help.

**[If you're ready for change on Long Island, chip in $5 or more now. We're running a people-powered campaign with folks like you chipping in what they can when they can.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528738/315858749/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=)**

Thank you so much, friend. I'm excited to embark on this quest for change together.

Kara Hahn

[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38528741/315858750/-1251159875?refcode=em21_welcome1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGNlMGQ4MS0wODQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lw76zVkkzhGDJo3h78xUa3zw7GjReZbOuWLhbsjcgFc=)

name: Letitia James
subject: ICYMI: I'm running to build a better New York
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James for NY
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38529079/315860328/324296880?refcode=email-header&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MjE1NGMzMi0wOTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SAJ9yG2fTrAG3hD3yvIAoCJ-Zipsi62c7HpFAjvVu48=)
---
Peter,

I'm Letitia James, and I'm running to be the next governor of the great state of New York.
I didn't grow up with power and privilege. And that's why I've dedicated my life to securing equality, justice, and progress for every last New Yorker – because I staunchly believe that no one should be treated worse than the neighbor on their right, nor better than the neighbor on their left. And I'm ready to fight for the change we need to build a better New York for all – but Peter, I cannot win this without you.
[**I'm personally reaching out to you today to help kickstart my campaign for governor by contributing $5 or more before midnight tonight. I am fiercely committed to the voices of my New York community, because this is a state of incredible opportunity. I should know: I’ve lived it.

My parents came to Brooklyn from the South to give me and my siblings the chance at a better life. That meant I could attend public schools that blessed me with a good education.
Still, as a Black girl growing up in Brooklyn, too many tried to count me out and write me off as a statistic. Then I made history by becoming the first Black woman to be elected to a statewide office in New York – but the truth is, that distinction is nothing more than a historical footnote if you don't do any good with the office.

I am so lucky to have been afforded the incredible opportunities this great state has given me – and I believe it's my duty to pay that forward. I want to fight for that opportunity for every last New Yorker. **I want to fight for you, Peter.**
**[Watch my campaign video and rush $5, $15, $50, or more before 11:59 p.m. – whatever you can spare to fuel the launch of this campaign – so I can make our vision for a more equal and just New York a reality. ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38529087/315860363/1687198019?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk200-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MjE1NGMzMi0wOTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SAJ9yG2fTrAG3hD3yvIAoCJ-Zipsi62c7HpFAjvVu48=)**

I can't tell you how much it means to me to have you on my team. Thank you.

Letitia James

name: Abby Finkenauer
subject: Today, I'm remembering my grandfather
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abby for Iowa Abby for Iowa
body:
| | |
---
Peter,

Today, on Veterans Day, I want to talk to you about how my late grandfather and his service – to his country and family – inspire me to this very day. I hope you'll give it a read and join me in honoring the men and women who have served in our military.
When the Jan. 6 insurrection on our Capitol happened, and I was watching our democracy and my friends being attacked in real time, I thought of all of our heroes who have stood up for our country and democracy and given everything they had to protect it. I wondered what Grandpa Finkenauer, who was wounded on the USS Bunker Hill and walked around with shrapnel in his leg until the day he died, would have thought of this moment.

But I didn’t wonder what he would have done. I knew.

He would have stood strong, he would never have given up hope and he would have continued to believe in and fight for the country and democracy he loved and that gave him an incredible life.
That’s who our veterans are. That’s what makes our country great, and that’s why no matter how much the hate, lies and misinformation try to divide us, we must never give up. We must honor our veterans' courage and love for our country by standing up and doing all we can to save it, together.

We must also recommit ourselves to being there for our heroes and their families, both now and in the future, when it comes to dignity, respect and their health care.

They gave us their best – they deserve ours.
**It's the memory of my grandfather and his service that inspires me to fight for our country and my community – no matter the cost. **

Today, as our nation pays tribute to the millions of veterans who have protected and served for us, I hope you'll join me in honoring their service – and I hope you feel inspired by a veteran in your life who, perhaps like my grandfather did, moves you to step up and serve in your own way.

To our veterans, thank you for your service. I fought for you in Congress, and I promise that I will continue to fight for you in the U.S. Senate.

– Abby

| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. To receive fewer emails, please click [here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38529088/315860365/496662621?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MzU5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTE1NGMzMi0wOTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qG7oKUUrO4nmLMUuMeE9Qz4YHub4DfTMGhZ2JFtFOC8=&emci=eebeb1f3-4b41-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=8e154c32-0943-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=13784334#!).

name: Jahana Hayes
subject: Honoring those who served
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
Today, we stop to appreciate our veterans. To really appreciate the sacrifices our veterans have made. To appreciate how they put their lives at risk. To appreciate how they left their families behind. To appreciate what they gave up in order to protect our country and our freedom. To our veterans: THANK YOU. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38529228/315860957/-1295403277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MDM4MTQ3OS0wOTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wWbybcd94dasIpao0BgU0Uk3tctrcie7ddi5Bg4wqAo=&emci=46d328be-fd42-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=40381479-0943-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=15529135) | Today, we stop to appreciate our veterans. To really appreciate the sacrifices our veterans have made. To appreciate how they put their lives at risk. To appreciate how they left their families behind. To appreciate what they gave up in order to protect our country and our freedom. To our veterans: THANK YOU. Every one of us is indebted to those who served our country. Our veterans – and their families – deserve so much more than just one day to commemorate their sacrifices and bravery. But a day dedicated to expressing our gratitude and appreciation is a good start. We have a responsibility to our veterans to provide the services they need after returning home. In Congress, I am committed to ensuring that all veterans have access to quality healthcare, a steady job, and a safe place to live. Yesterday, as Chair of the Subcommittee in Nutrition, Oversight and Department Operations, I convened a hearing to examine the issue of food insecurity among veterans. I am committed to working towards solutions to this problem. This Veterans Day, I am asking you to join me in honoring and remembering our veterans for everything they and their families have done to protect our lives, our freedom, and our country. Today and every day – thank you. Now, let’s show our appreciation by taking care of them. – Jahana
---

This Veterans Day, I am asking you to join me in honoring and remembering our veterans for everything they and their families have done to protect our lives, our freedom, and our country. Today and every day – thank you. Now, let’s show our appreciation by taking care of them. – Jahana * Email is an important way for us to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. If you'd like to receive fewer emails, . If you need to update your contact information, . If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . | Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes Friends of Jahana Hayes P.O. Box 1487 Waterbury, CT 06721 United States

name: Pastor Eric Gash
subject: Honoring our Veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Gash for Congress
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38529954/315863074/-851526706?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDg3OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYmQ1MDU3My0wYTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DByHByP58RUo5jcagJ39L5jsUauA5j2c02bkpeb_mwM=)
Happy Veterans Day, friend.

Today, we honor the brave men and women who risked their lives in service to our country. Because of their sacrifices, we have the privilege of living in a peaceful and democratic nation.

We also know that when a veteran chooses to serve, it's not their burden alone. It’s also their family -- their parents, spouses, and children who must sacrifice.

We still have much more to do in repaying our debt to our veterans. Whether it’s making sure they have access to the physical and mental health care they need, or making sure they have a roof above their heads. In Congress, that's a commitment I promise to uphold for our veterans in North Carolina and across the country.

In service,

Pastor Eric Gash

name: Kathy Barnette
subject: Happy Veterans Day
body:
---
| | | | | | |
---
[ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001VIv0Dk7yd57cQUqmQCZcE1xcFQ_b9LtaatjfVkGG7WPgvzsymNEFT9OdHB93cTQaCfFRxe7c1L8OsFZxPLF3dCdR7ggSi5Q5dFsTOxrGaqxE-rV_QjFhLFKP9J9XW7Up9USoDQR1XChSv6qIS7Bd0g==&c=T5TKaLir9yfPzik89lcThEYrqT4CqjjKV9HALm4xQW9Y5C7zRT1WGA==&ch=1SXTy8wN08alEjnoYecKKCkV-fqGR2seBnJZOc8XBV-7xwSZGEZr5Q==)
---
|
---
| Peter,
---
| Our most precious resources in this country are the brave men and women who fight to keep us free!
This Veterans Day, join me in thanking all the troops who protect the great land we all call home, especially those who paid the ultimate price and gave their lives to keep you and me free.
As a veteran myself, I know what our fighting men and women go through in order to defend America. Whether by land, air, sea, or space, our service members have given us the ability to live the American dream!
---
|
---
| Any country owes its veterans a debt of thanks, but a republic founded on freedom and opportunity owes its soldiers that much more for protecting those ideals. Any less than the absolute best for our veterans is a disgrace. That's why, as your next Senator, I will do everything in my power to ensure that our military receives the support it needs to remain the greatest fighting force in the history of the world, and our veterans receive the support they deserve for their invaluable service.
So I wish you and yours a very happy Veterans Day. And to every single man and woman who has answered the call to service for your country - thank you.
---
|
---
| Kathy Barnette
---
| | |
---
| Kathy Barnette for Senate | [Website](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001VIv0Dk7yd57cQUqmQCZcE1xcFQ_b9LtaatjfVkGG7WPgvzsymNEFT5PS_zWiA_d2nVMI9r6OYyyWzJWlXBlqfPdSQoQ7C1T6rh0Vt6KYSt5jxbPS5fumGNZ5dSelg2yrNjt65IYTGp62q46aa3I_Zg==&c=T5TKaLir9yfPzik89lcThEYrqT4CqjjKV9HALm4xQW9Y5C7zRT1WGA==&ch=1SXTy8wN08alEjnoYecKKCkV-fqGR2seBnJZOc8XBV-7xwSZGEZr5Q==)

name: Tom Nelson
subject: Honoring our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin
body:
# [#]Today, we honor the incredible contributions and sacrifices veterans have made for our country. They have selflessly put everything on the line in order to protect and strengthen our American values, and for that, we thank them.

Having 10 veterans in my own family, I’m familiar with the hardships that come from serving. While today is dedicated to commemorating our veterans, we shouldn’t stop here — many of our former service members struggle economically, mentally, or physically and have been left behind by politicians in DC. I’m determined to change that.

I can promise you this: I won’t just honor our veterans on November 11th. I’ll honor them everyday by fighting for policies that will support them and give them the resources they need to live healthy, happy lives.

Today and always, we thank our veterans.

Onward,

Tom
VOLUNTEER » [https://go.nelsonforwi.com/a/pledge-to-volunteer]
Tom Nelson serves as County Executive for Outagamie, one of the worst-hit COVID areas in the nation. Local officials have been leading our country through the pandemic because Donald Trump and Ron Johnson have failed. Tom is running for U.S. Senate because we need real leaders in Washington who will take COVID seriously. And if we can take on a pandemic, we can take on anything. And if you can win in a red county six times, you can win anywhere. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. name: Sheriff David Clarke
subject: Kyle Rittenhouse needs us. Now.
body:
| | | | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58743077:6PmnDX2QN:m:1:3253819041:0061BC7543776F66555A7874FCE454E2:r)
---
Peter,

From Day 1, the case against Kyle Rittenhouse has been **political, malicious and built upon outright lies.**

And now that trial has begun, it's starting to fall apart piece by piece.

**The good news is this, Kyle can 100% win this case and in doing so, safeguard the right of self-defense for you, me and every American.**

But the cost of his legal defense is immense, and they've now had to bring on extra security to deal with a growing slew of death threats.

I've been helping his mother Wendy raise money but we still aren't where we need to be.

**[So I'm asking you to dig deep now. Kyle needs your generous support and he needs it today. At this moment. I cannot emphasize it enough.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58743077:6PmnDX2QN:m:1:3253819041:0061BC7543776F66555A7874FCE454E2:r)**
|
---
[DONATE NOW >>](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58743077:6PmnDX2QN:m:1:3253819041:0061BC7543776F66555A7874FCE454E2:r)
|
---
For 15 of my nearly 40 year career in law enforcement I served right near Kenosha as Sheriff of Milwaukee County, Wisconsin. I know the area well.

What was done to that city last summer by a bunch of violent lunatics was an absolute disgrace. Kyle was there that night, you better believe it.

But instead of starting fires, he had a fire extinguisher trying to put them out.

Instead of trying to hurt people, he had a medical kit offering first aid to anyone who needed it.

And instead of yelling at our TV or computer getting angry about this obvious miscarriage of justice, you and I are going to help.

**We're going to make sure Kyle gets the legal defense he deserves.**

**[So please donate to FreeKyleUSA now. It could not possibly be more urgent.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/58743077:6PmnDX2QN:m:1:3253819041:0061BC7543776F66555A7874FCE454E2:r)**

Thank you.

Steadfast,

Sheriff David A. Clarke (Ret.)
|
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Captain=20Mike=20for=20Governor?=
subject: Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michael Brown for Governor • P
body:
Throughout history, many brave men and women have served, and many serve today - here stateside and in far away places. Today is their day. | | | |
---
| | |
---
|
---
|
---
| Peter:

Today is the day we honor all who served our nation. I received my start in life on my 18th birthday when I enlisted and served four years in the Marine Corps infantry. It was a time I am truly grateful for.

Throughout history, many brave men and women have served, and many serve today here stateside and in far away places. Today is their day. Please remember that.

Below is an excerpt from President Ronald Reagan’s speech at the Veterans Day Wreath-Laying Ceremony at Arlington National Cemetery in 1985:

_... A few moments ago I placed a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, and as I stepped back and stood during the moment of silence that followed, I said a small prayer. And it occurred to me that each of my predecessors has had a similar moment, and I wondered if our prayers weren't very much the same, if not identical.

We celebrate Veterans Day on the anniversary of the armistice that ended World War I, the armistice that began on the 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month. And I wonder, in fact, if all Americans' prayers aren't the same as those I mentioned a moment ago. The timing of this holiday is quite deliberate in terms of historical fact but somehow it always seems quite fitting to me that this day comes deep in autumn when the colors are muted and the days seem to invite contemplation..._ _Perhaps we can start by remembering this: that all of those who died for us and our country were, in one way or another, victims of a peace process that failed; victims of a decision to forget certain things; to forget, for instance, that the surest way to keep a peace going is to stay strong. Weakness, after all, is a temptation — it tempts the pugnacious to assert themselves — but strength is a declaration that cannot be misunderstood. Strength is a condition that declares actions have consequences. Strength is a prudent warning to the belligerent that aggression need not go unanswered._ _Peace fails when we forget what we stand for. It fails when we forget that our Republic is based on firm principles, principles that have real meaning, that with them, we are the last, best hope of man on Earth; without them, we're little more than the crust of a continent. Peace also fails when we forget to bring to the bargaining table God’s first intellectual gift to man: common sense. Common sense gives us a realistic knowledge of human beings and how they think, how they live in the world, what motivates them. Common sense tells us that man has magic in him, but also clay. Common sense can tell the difference between right and wrong. Common sense forgives error, but it always recognizes it to be error first._ _We endanger the peace and confuse all issues when we obscure the truth; when we refuse to name an act for what it is; when we refuse to see the obvious and seek safety in Almighty. Peace is only maintained and won by those who have clear eyes and brave minds. Peace is imperiled when we forget to try for agreements and settlements and treaties; when we forget to hold out our hands and strive; when we forget that God gave us talents to use in securing the ends He desires. Peace fails when we forget that agreements, once made, cannot be broken without a price._ _Each new day carries within it the potential for breakthroughs, for progress. Each new day bursts with possibilities. And so, hope is realistic and despair a pointless little sin. And peace fails when we forget to pray to the source of all peace and life and happiness. I think sometimes of General Matthew Ridgeway, who, the night before D-day, tossed sleepless on his cot and talked to the Lord and listened for the promise that God made to Joshua: “I will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.”_ _We're surrounded today by the dead of our wars. We owe them a debt we can never repay. All we can do is remember them and what they did and why they had to be brave for us. All we can do is try to see that other young men never have to join them. Today, as never before, we must pledge to remember the things that will continue the peace. Today, as never before, we must pray for God's help in broadening and deepening the peace we enjoy. Let us pray for freedom and justice and a more stable world. And let us make a compact today with the dead, a promise in the words for which General Ridgeway listened, “I will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.”_ _In memory of those who gave the last full measure of devotion, may our efforts to achieve lasting peace gain strength. And through whatever coincidence or accident of timing, I tell you that a week from now when I am some thousands of miles away, believe me, the memory and the importance of this day will be in the forefront of my mind and in my heart._ _Thank you. God bless you all, and God bless America._ You can watch the full speech here:

name: Derek Kilmer
subject: I won't stop fighting until all veterans have the care and benefits they need
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
Dear Peter,

Happy Veterans Day -- particularly to those who served our nation. Our nation is stronger and our world is safer because of the men and women who served. One day a year cannot adequately thank them for their service. In fact, we should demonstrate our gratitude every day.

Saying thank you is important, but showing our thanks is even better. That's why honoring our nation's veterans is one of my top priorities in Congress. **That means we should work to ensure every veteran has a home to sleep in, the care they need -- and have earned -- and the opportunities and benefits that their families were promised.**

In the past few weeks, I've been proud to support several pieces of legislation to support our nation's veterans. From bipartisan bills to make the VA more accessible to legislation to help end veteran suicide to initiatives focused on ensuring every veteran has a job -- these bills are the very least our government can do to repay those who've put their lives on the line for this country.

America didn't become the strong nation it is today without the commitment and sacrifice of our nation's veterans, and our freedom has continued because of their service.

**No words can adequately express the debt of gratitude we owe veterans and their families, but we can all start by saying "thank you" and by committing to having their backs the way they've had ours.**

It's an honor to serve you all,

Derek

name: Derek Marshall
subject: Stories from the district
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Derek Marshall for Congress
body:
Peter,
There’s something you may not know about California’s 8th Congressional district. It’s the largest district in California and even one of the biggest districts in the whole country. **As the district lines stand now, it would take 6 hours to drive the length of the district.** How do I know that? Because I have!

Before I announced my bid for Congress at the beginning of this year, I spent several weeks driving through the district and learning about many of the small towns and rural communities that make up the Northern portion of the district. Out of the district’s 700,000+ residents, 500,000 of them live within one hour of the Victor Valley, where I live. **But the rural towns count too, and their residents shouldn’t go unnoticed and underserved simply because of their location. **

**[I’m running for Congress to represent all of California’s 8th district, because no one should be ignored simply for where they live. I’m building a grassroots movement that will serve us all. Can you chip in and help us get to Washington?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38533873/315871732/-1799966630?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWVlYmJlMS0xMDQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hhtpp1_uJ7rf0u8n4KTPsturjcu4MuQuffo934r-MjI=)**

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38533878/315871743/-1799966630?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWVlYmJlMS0xMDQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hhtpp1_uJ7rf0u8n4KTPsturjcu4MuQuffo934r-MjI=)

**Yesterday, I drove 4 hours round trip to go speak with members of the Needles Democratic Club, who shared with me many stories of the poor governance they suffer.** We met in a pizza parlor and talked for hours about life in the town and the solutions they desperately need.
**Because there’s little to no oversight of the local government, corruption is running rampant among so-called public servants.** The city council is lining its pockets with help from corporate interests and kickbacks. Meanwhile, the town is full of crumbling infrastructure. The portion of Historic Route 66 that goes through the town—which could be a helpful draw for the town—is in disrepair with no plans to revive it.

Residents of Needles have to cross the border into Arizona to reach the nearest grocery store, because there isn’t one in the whole town. It’s extremely difficult to access good medical care with no local hospital. And it’s hard to get connected on the internet without consistent broadband. **The picture they painted demonstrated the failures of their elected officials, but it doesn’t have to stay that way. **

I pledged to the folks I met that as Congressman, I would fight for more resources to rural towns like theirs. I pledged I would use my influence and position to hold local officials accountable. I pledged that I would not forget the stories they entrusted me with. **Because unlike Representative Jay Obernolte, I would use my time in Congress to actually represent my constituents, instead of the corporate interests that are often responsible for the conditions they endure. **

**[This district deserves a Congressman that works every day without fail to make sure their needs are being met. It’s time for Republican Obernolte to go, so we can finally get to work restoring our homes and lives. Can you contribute today to help me replace him?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38533881/315871757/-1799966630?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWVlYmJlMS0xMDQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hhtpp1_uJ7rf0u8n4KTPsturjcu4MuQuffo934r-MjI=)**

In Solidarity,

**Derek Marshall**
Candidate for U.S. Congress, CA-08

name: Kirsten Engel
subject: Honoring our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Engel for Arizona
body:
http://engelforarizona.com [http://engelforarizona.com]

Friend,

As we commemorate this Veterans Day, I wanted to take a moment to talk about how we can best honor the special and sacred commitment our country makes to those who serve.

Our veterans took an oath to defend our country. We have a responsibility to hold up our end of the bargain and make sure they have the services and support they need after they've finished their service.

That's why I am committed to honoring their sacrifice by championing the issues that impact our veteran community most:

*
Modernizing
VA
healthcare
facilities
and
creating
a
welcoming
VA
for
all
veterans,
ensuring
that
VA
benefits
are
provided
equitably
to
all
who
have
served.

*
Expanding
mental
health
services
to
reduce
the
number
of
veteran
suicides
and
support
our
most
at-risk
veterans,
including
women,
LGBTQ+,
and
Native
Americans.

*
Investing
in
rural
broadband
to
expand
the
availability
of
telehealth
services.

*
Addressing
toxic
exposures
for
all
veterans
and
passing
the
Presumptive
Benefits
for
War
Fighters
Exposed
to
Burn
Pits
and
Other
Toxins
Act
of
2021.

No veteran should struggle to find a job, a home, or feed their family. Nor should our veterans be forced to spend an inordinate amount of time trying to get the health care they need and deserve.

Congress can do good things, but it requires doing the work of listening to people, truly caring about them, and putting in the elbow grease to get results.

I hope you will join me in fighting to keep our promises to our nation’s veterans.

Thank you,

Kirsten

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP01/1/62709/images/Kirsten%20signatures.png]

Paid for by Engel for Arizona

Engel for Arizona
PO Box 40721
Tucson, AZ 85717
United States

Our veterans took an oath to defend our country. We have a responsibility to hold up our end of the bargain and make sure they have the services and support they need after they've finished their service. That's why I am committed to honoring their sacrifice by championing the issues that impact our veteran community most: Modernizing VA healthcare facilities and creating a welcoming VA for all veterans, ensuring that VA benefits are provided equitably to all who have served. Expanding mental health services to reduce the number of veteran suicides and support our most at-risk veterans, including women, LGBTQ+, and Native Americans. Investing in rural broadband to expand the availability of telehealth services. Congress can do good things, but it requires doing the work of listening to people, truly caring about them, and putting in the elbow grease to get results. I hope you will join me in fighting to keep our promises to our nation’s veterans. | | Peter,

On Veterans Day, we take time to honor and thank the brave veterans who sacrificed so much to protect our freedoms.

While we can never fully repay our veterans for what they have done for our country, we live up to the promises we made them.

That means providing our veterans — and their families — with quality health care, affordable housing, and good-paying job and education opportunities.

To our veterans across Colorado, thank you for your service and sacrifice.

Dr. Yadira Caraveo

name: Mark Takano
subject: We need to keep our promises to our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P
body:
| Peter, today is Veterans Day -- a time to honor U.S. military veterans for everything they have done and sacrificed to protect our nation’s freedom, values and interests around the world.

As such, our government has a duty to ensure veterans receive the care and benefits they deserve.

And as Chairman of the Veterans Affairs Committee in Congress, I'm so proud to be working every day to honor the work of our veterans and make sure that we as a country keep our promises to them.

That means ensuring veterans have access to healthcare, housing, and education opportunities, creating a special focus on veteran mental health and preventing veteran suicide, protecting veterans from being exploited by extremist groups, and so much more.

Today and always I am committed to supporting our veterans. I hope you’ll join me on this national holiday in paying respect to our brave veterans.

Sincerely,

Mark Takano | [DONATE »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534515/315874620/232128462?amount=3&refcode=MTFC_20211111_EMINFO_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYWRkN2U5NC0xMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X2tTdOnlM1rWCM3RmAcqqCwyH9klQ3YUr5yQQBuEKDA=)

name: Michelle Lujan Grisham
subject: Honoring our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by New Mexicans for Michelle, John Wertheim, Treasurer
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534516/315874641/-1540418300?refcode=jtk1094-ot-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzc2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZmRkN2U5NC0xMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IKKKTA3hW9a1knIG34X7MKpMoBcH4amzIJSDtA5PFfM=)
---
Peter,

Veterans Day is an important reminder to show our appreciation for the brave service members who have sacrificed for our nation. On this day and everyday we remember their bravery to stand up for the American people and protect the freedoms that we enjoy.
**The service members who answered our nation's call to serve represent the very best of our American values:** They've shown selflessness in the face of danger, readiness to step up to help others, and an unwavering commitment to our country.
**Our state is home to more than 130,000 veterans, and I know each of these heroes has their own story of honor, duty, and sacrifice.** To each of New Mexico's veterans, I am endlessly thankful for your sacrifice.
Our service members leave behind families when they are deployed, and I am so thankful for the sacrifices that these spouses, children, and parents make at home while their loved ones are away serving our country.
**We can never fully repay the debt we owe our nation's veterans and their families, but I hope we start by showing these selfless Americans the respect they deserve on this day of remembrance. **
To all of our veterans and their families, I can't thank you enough for your service.

Sincerely,

Gov. Lujan Grisham
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/10c4825d-a741-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8/cfdd7e94-1143-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzc2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZmRkN2U5NC0xMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IKKKTA3hW9a1knIG34X7MKpMoBcH4amzIJSDtA5PFfM=&id=127162320) | | Paid for by New Mexicans for Michelle, John Wertheim, Treasurer

name: Ras Smith
subject: Veterans Day.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ras Smith for Governor
body:
Let's make it our mission to ask how we can best serve veterans. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534596/315874818/692104027?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOWM2NWQ2Ni0xMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6C72fD4ZxSlSjz_TGrpKgXS_GktHcokXiXaYn4O2OhU=)
Happy Veterans Day, friend.

Last night, I hosted a roundtable with a handful of veterans to discuss how we can better serve and make Iowa a more welcoming place for veterans.

I want to thank Army Reservist Jeno Berta, Navy Veteran Ryan Zeskey, Admiral Mike Franken, and my mother, Belinda Creighton-Smith, for joining our discussion.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534606/315874828/-229712856?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOWM2NWQ2Ni0xMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6C72fD4ZxSlSjz_TGrpKgXS_GktHcokXiXaYn4O2OhU=&emci=45c07e93-0f43-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=39c65d66-1143-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=21044445) | **[View the roundtable →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534612/315874836/-229712856?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOWM2NWQ2Ni0xMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6C72fD4ZxSlSjz_TGrpKgXS_GktHcokXiXaYn4O2OhU=&emci=45c07e93-0f43-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=39c65d66-1143-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=21044445)**
---

I come from a long line of veterans. Mom served in the Army Reserves, and my grandfather was in the Marines. They instilled in me the values of sacrifice and service to our communities and country -- part of why I ran for the state legislature in the first place and am now running for governor.

The main thing I heard last night was the need for better funding and support for service members and veterans. We shouldn't wait for them to ask for help -- we need to provide better mental and physical health care, adequate housing, and empower them in the workforce.

I'm excited to be running for governor to work with service members and veterans and make sure that we do right by them and their families.

Thanks,

Ras Smith

clean_body: Let's make it our mission to ask how we can best serve veterans. | = Happy Veterans Day, friend. Last night, I hosted a roundtable with a handful of veterans to discuss how we can better serve and make Iowa a more welcoming place for veterans. I want to thank Army Reservist Jeno Berta, Navy Veteran Ryan Zeskey, Admiral Mike Franken, and my mother, Belinda Creighton-Smith, for joining our discussion. | I come from a long line of veterans. Mom served in the Army Reserves, and my grandfather was in the Marines. They instilled in me the values of sacrifice and service to our communities and country -- part of why I ran for the state legislature in the first place and am now running for governor. The main thing I heard last night was the need for better funding and support for service members and veterans. We shouldn't wait for them to ask for help -- we need to provide better mental and physical health care, adequate housing, and empower them in the workforce. I'm excited to be running for governor to work with service members and veterans and make sure that we do right by them and their families. Thanks, Ras Smith = | | Paid for by Ras Smith for Governor Ras Smith for Governor P. O. name: Martin Heinrich
subject: Keeping our promises
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534682/315874915/-272948131?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZWRkN2U5NC0xMTQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VfpAwHQ30qeL0yfQEA0fnQnbeveecam-Rni-BoHCYJc=&emci=41d98498-9642-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=cedd7e94-1143-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=252045)
---
Hello — Today is Veterans Day — a day when we honor those who **answered the call to defend our nation.** I’m reflecting on the sacrifices of all of our veterans — including the Navajo code talkers and Bataan Death March survivors who sacrificed so much for New Mexico and our country in World War II. And I’m thinking of my father, a veteran and a lineman. **If you or a loved one has served, thank you.** I promise that I will keep working as Chair of the Military Construction and Veterans Affairs Appropriations Subcommittee to ensure our men and women in uniform have what they need to keep us safe — and receive the support they need after their service. Because our servicemembers and veterans deserve more than our words. They deserve the best health care, benefits, and opportunities available. We have made groundbreaking investments in veterans’ health care — including in rural and hard-to-reach communities. And we’ve made big gains in providing homeless assistance, transportation, and more. But we won’t stop there. We as a nation have promised to take care of our veterans. I will always work to fulfill that promise. Thank you. — Martin
---
| Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
---
Senator Martin Heinrich is committed to helping New Mexico become a leader in defense, tech, and energy with opportunities in every corner. name: Tom O'Halleran
subject: Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
Thank you to those who serve.
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534794/315875662/-1425992037?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjM2MxYmM4ZS0xMjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bixLicQr0NJAIninlqG3yq9A2iDCwIx4aVTubxjyQxY=&emci=3f14fba0-8541-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=c3c1bc8e-1243-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=18634996)** Folks, today, I am reaching out to say thank you to those who have put everything on the line to serve our country. Veterans Day is a time for us to recognize the sacrifices made by those who have served in the military, and our team stands united in respect for all our service members. November is Military Family Appreciation Month. Military families often face unique challenges and sacrifices, and we must continue to recognize the crucial support they provide their family members who serve. Our service members and their families deserve the very best our country has to offer. So, I hope you’ll join me today as I thank our veterans for protecting our country’s freedom and security, and continue to seek out ways we can support them and their families during and after their time in the military. Thank you, Congressman Tom O’Halleran
---

name: Wes Moore
subject: On this Veterans Day
body:
| | | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534871/315876098/-1916349277?source=20211111_em_pet_b1_moore_30cl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjM0MWFmYS0xMjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QZzrB04RwNg6SeUqQb2tJ68t-54mY99c-itjaUCPuow=&emci=3ee97faf-7042-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=cf341afa-1243-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=24635485)
---

I served as an Army Paratrooper and a captain, leading soldiers in combat in Afghanistan.
This Veterans Day marks a time when we need service-driven and battle-tested leadership, now more than ever.
I have lived my life by a basic principle ingrained in me by the U.S. Army -- leave no one behind.
That's the reason I'm running for Governor of Maryland.
[_**If you’re a veteran, will you please stand with me and sign up for our Veterans for Wes group? >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534872/315876099/-1916349277?source=20211111_em_pet_b1_moore_30cl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjM0MWFmYS0xMjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QZzrB04RwNg6SeUqQb2tJ68t-54mY99c-itjaUCPuow=&emci=3ee97faf-7042-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=cf341afa-1243-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=24635485)
[_**And if you’re not a veteran but you support electing more people who have served, please sign up to endorse me here >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534873/315876100/-1689863357?source=20211111_em_pet_b1_moore_30cl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjM0MWFmYS0xMjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QZzrB04RwNg6SeUqQb2tJ68t-54mY99c-itjaUCPuow=&emci=3ee97faf-7042-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=cf341afa-1243-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=24635485)
Today we honor our service members.
Their stories, their service, and their sacrifice.
To be a veteran isn’t a designation of something we did in our past. It’s a marker of who we are.
We are public servants who have a shared bond, who always have each other’s backs, and who stepped up to lead because we were asked to.
So many of us feel the calling to step up. To serve. To lead.
[**And that’s why I’m running to be the next Governor of Maryland: To use the leadership ability I gained in the Army to build a state where we leave no one behind.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534874/315876101/-1689863357?source=20211111_em_pet_b1_moore_30cl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjM0MWFmYS0xMjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QZzrB04RwNg6SeUqQb2tJ68t-54mY99c-itjaUCPuow=&emci=3ee97faf-7042-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=cf341afa-1243-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=24635485)
[_**If you’re a veteran, will you please sign up for our Veterans for Wes group? >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534875/315876102/-1916349277?source=20211111_em_pet_b1_moore_30cl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjM0MWFmYS0xMjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QZzrB04RwNg6SeUqQb2tJ68t-54mY99c-itjaUCPuow=&emci=3ee97faf-7042-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=cf341afa-1243-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=24635485)
[_**And if you’re not a veteran but you support electing more people who have served to office, please sign up to endorse me here >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/38534876/315876103/-1689863357?source=20211111_em_pet_b1_moore_30cl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjM0MWFmYS0xMjQzLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QZzrB04RwNg6SeUqQb2tJ68t-54mY99c-itjaUCPuow=&emci=3ee97faf-7042-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&emdi=cf341afa-1243-ec11-9820-c896653b26c8&ceid=24635485)
Happy Veterans Day to all who have served, sacrificed, and answered the call to lead.

Elevate,

Wes

---

He’s running to become Governor of Maryland and flip this state blue for the first time in 8 years. Wes believes no matter where you start in life, you deserve an equal opportunity to succeed. We know you receive a lot of emails -- but that’s because winning this race is going to take a massive grassroots campaign. We need to educate, empower, and register thousands of new Democrats. clean_body: | | | I served as an Army Paratrooper and a captain, leading soldiers in combat in Afghanistan. This Veterans Day marks a time when we need service-driven and battle-tested leadership, now more than ever. I have lived my life by a basic principle ingrained in me by the U.S. Army -- leave no one behind. That's the reason I'm running for Governor of Maryland. Today we honor our service members. Their stories, their service, and their sacrifice. To be a veteran isn’t a designation of something we did in our past. It’s a marker of who we are. We are public servants who have a shared bond, who always have each other’s backs, and who stepped up to lead because we were asked to. So many of us feel the calling to step up. To serve. To lead. Happy Veterans Day to all who have served, sacrificed, and answered the call to lead. Elevate, Wes Wes Moore is a combat veteran, a former small business owner, and the former CEO of a leading non-profit. He’s running to become Governor of Maryland and flip this state blue for the first time in 8 years. Wes believes no matter where you start in life, you deserve an equal opportunity to succeed. We know you receive a lot of emails -- but that’s because winning this race is going to take a massive grassroots campaign. We need to educate, empower, and register thousands of new Democrats. We need to turn out every voter in every corner of Maryland. And we need your help to do it. | | = Making your gift monthly will maximize the resources to help Wes bring change to Maryland. Make your donation monthly right now! | = It’s supporters like you who fuel Wes’ campaign and we’re so thankful for your support. We’re committed to ensuring your contact information is up to date and accurate. If you are not dpwillis67@gmail.com, please update your preferred name and email . Any contact information here reflects the donor information we have on file for this specific email. If you donated with a spouse, with a check, or with a different email address, it may not be reflected in our records just yet. Thank you so much for your support and understanding while we work to update our records. If you’d prefer to receive fewer emails, we understand.
